channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Feminist Foreign Policies Ride the Global Progressive Movement
item.description: For progressive movements, foreign policy has emerged as a major site of contestation, increasingly swaying votes in national elections, drawing mass attention and fueling public debate. Among the most intriguing levers of the progressive foreign policy movement — which prioritizes diplomacy and the protection of human rights over military intervention — is the continuing effort ... Read more

item.title: Global AI Governance Cannot Wait As the Digital Divide Widens
item.description: Throughout July 2026, various United Nations (UN) bodies and global experts have underscored the need for increased comprehensive artificial intelligence (AI) governance. Although AI yields significant potential gains for global development, persistent structural barriers continue to impede equitable access and deepen the digital divide. From July 6 to 7, the UN convened the first session […]

item.title: Changing Age Distributions of Populations
item.description: In many countries, older adults (aged 65 and above) now outnumber younger individuals (under the age of 18), reflecting a significant demographic transformation known as demographic ageing. The shift in a population’s age structure is becoming increasingly common around the world and is primarily driven by declining fertility rates and rising life expectancy. As populations […]

item.title: LIVE: Haiti endures violence and hunger, but hopes grow for a more secure future
item.description: After years of violence perpetrated by gangs, widespread insecurity and the displacement of almost 1.5 million people as well as growing humanitarian needs, Haitians are glimpsing signs of hope as a path to democratic elections begins to emerge. At UN Headquarters in New York, the 15-member Security Council discusses what next for Haiti. Full live coverage below.

item.title: Moon Day focuses growing concerns around space junk
item.description: Amid surging lunar exploration, the UN on Monday urged governments to ensure that humanity's return to the Moon does not come at the expense of planet Earth – or the long-term sustainability of space.

item.title: ‘I consider it a miracle’: Pregnant woman pulled from post-quake rubble in Venezuela
item.description: When a 12-story apartment building pancaked in La Guaira, Venezuela, following two powerful earthquakes late last month, panic ensued to save people injured or trapped under the rubble.

item.title: Ukraine: Deadly Russian attacks severely damage aid warehouses
item.description: Russian attacks and drone strikes killed at least two children and injured dozens more across Ukraine over recent days while severely damaging three critical aid warehouses, UN agencies reported on Monday.

item.title: Middle East: Civilians again under fire as US-Iran strikes continue
item.description: As the United States and Iran trade deadly attacks for a second week, the UN continues to call for de-escalation while highlighting the devastation caused to civilians and critical infrastructure across the Middle East.

item.title: Efficiency Becomes the World’s ‘First Fuel’ as Leaders at IEA Conference Chart Course for Cleaner, More Secure Energy Future
item.description: As geopolitical tensions, rising electricity demand and climate pressures reshape the global energy landscape, more than 600 energy ministers, chief executives, financial leaders and policy experts gathered in Montreal, deliberating upon how to deliver at an unprecedented scale. The 11th Annual Global Conference on Energy Efficiency, organised by the International Energy Agency (IEA) on July […]

item.title: Communities vs. Datacentres
item.description: Few things unite Donald Trump’s most ardent supporters with the Democratic Party’s progressive wing, but datacentres are one of them. Across the USA, communities are joining forces across the political divide to oppose developments few want but governments seem determined to impose. Michigan residents are among those who’ve come together across party lines to oppose […]

item.title: Palestine Must Not Disappear
item.description: A statement by Mary Robinson, Graça Machel, Helen Clark and Hina Jilani following their visit to the Middle East, 11-16 July 2026.
